title: Volume 1 Chapter 4: Christina of the 'Roaring Thunder'
content: Chapter 4: Christina of the 'Roaring Thunder'
An incredibly boring class finally ended, and it was after school.
It happened as I was walking across the academy grounds, heading home.
"Hey you, haven't you been getting a little carried away lately?"
Suddenly, I heard a line so stereotypical it almost felt scripted. Turning my gaze in that direction, I saw two gyaru-type girls cornering a single, timid-looking girl.
"N-No... I haven't been getting carried away..."
"That's exactly what I'm talking about, acting like such a good little girl... Ha!"
One of the gyaru girls forcefully snatched the timid girl's bag and slammed it onto the ground.
Tears welled up in the timid girl's eyes.
"Kyahaha, we went too far—that's seriously hilarious!"
Cackling with vulgar laughter, the two gyarus looked down on the girl.
Huh... So even this world has things like bullying, huh... Pretty realistic...
"Hey, you two over there."
Just then, a voice echoed from somewhere.
"Eh? Who's that?"
The moment the two gyarus looked around, a single girl descended from the sky.
The girl landed gracefully on the spot and glared fiercely at the two bullies.
Oh! That's...!!
"Geh... Ayano Kujō..."
The two gyarus grimaced, clearly displeased.
She's hereeeeeee!!
I mentally pumped my fist at the girl's arrival.
That girl, Ayano Kujō, is quite famous at this academy. A cool gyaru, she's basically a loner who constantly radiates a 'don't talk to me' aura, rejecting anyone who tries to get close.
And above all else... she is also a magical girl.

Her name as a magical girl is Christina. At first glance, she seems like an extremely cold person, but she's completely different. To sum it up in a single word, Christina is an extreme tsundere. Even though she usually acts coldly towards her comrades, she actually cherishes them more deeply than anyone else. That is Christina's charm.
Still, as expected of a magical girl... Even untransformed, her aura is leaps and bounds beyond a normal person's...!
"What are you doing?"
"Nothing, really..."
"N-Nothing at all..."
"Oh? ...Because to me, it looked like you were bullying this girl."
Overwhelmed by her intense pressure, the two gyarus instinctively stepped back.
"If you have the guts to bully someone... then you're prepared for the consequences yourself, right?"
Christina took a step closer.
"Tch... let's go!"
"You better remember this, Ayano Kujō!"
And just like that, the two gyarus were safely repelled by Christina.
"...As if I'd remember trash like you."
Spitting out those words, Christina picked up the girl's bag that had been thrown to the ground.
"Here. Try not to drop it next time."
"Th-Thank you... Kujō-san..."
"I didn't really do anything. Go on, shouldn't you be heading home?"
"Y-Yeah... Thank you so much!"
The girl bowed deeply and hurried off, looking much more energetic than before.
Man... I can't believe I got to witness a scene like that today... For the first time today, I was actually glad I came to school.
Well then, I saw what I needed to see. Let's hurry up and go ho—
"You over there. What have you been staring at this whole time?"
What... the...? How did she notice me...? My stealth skills as a mob character should be world-class...
"Did you really think I hadn't noticed?"
This is bad... Having a conversation with the magical girl Christina is terrible for my status as a mob... Alright, I'm going to run.
"You don't seriously think you can run away right now, do you?"
Just as I tried to flee, Christina, who had somehow gotten behind me, firmly grabbed my shoulder.
Ow, ow, ow. Christina-san, that hurts...
"If I remember correctly, you're in the same class... What was your name again?"
"It's not a name worth introducing..."
"Hurry up and name yourself."
"...It's Sōta Hayami."
"Hmm. Got it, Hayami. I'll remember it for now, though I might forget it later... Anyway, then—"
Right after that, I was backed up against the wall.
[img]https://i.imgur.com/R9E0XEP.jpeg
"Now that you've seen a side of me like that... I suppose I have no choice but to erase you..."
"Hold on, that's terrifying!"
It was a remark so unfitting for a magical girl, an ally of justice, that I couldn't help but retort naturally.
"Relax, obviously I'm not going to take your life. I'll just knock you out and erase your recent memories."
"That's still terrifying!"
Yep. This is undoubtedly Christina.
This sharp, abrasive personality is exactly as she was in the anime, perfectly matching my interpretation of her... Ah... it's the best...
"...Whatever, it's fine. I won't erase your memories."
"Then—"
"—Instead... you're going to keep me company for a bit."
"Swish!"
"Whoa!?"
I managed to dodge the intense punch thrown by Christina by a hair's breadth.
That was way too close... Were you seriously trying to blow my face off just now!?
"Hey, what are you gonna do if I get hurt!"
"If you have the free time to say that, you better keep your guard up. Otherwise you really will get hurt, you know?"
Christina laughed provocatively.
Christina told me to keep her company, but... why did she bring me to a boxing gym!?
Every single one of her strikes carries massive power. If one of them hits me, it feels like it would straight up break my bones, so I absolutely do not want to get hit.
As expected of the one with the best athletic reflexes among the magical girls.
"Can I go home now? I'm tired."
"No. My stress relief isn't over yet."
"So I really am just stress relief!"
"Yep. So be a good boy and let me beat you up... alright!"
In an instant, Christina's roundhouse kick blew right past my face.
I managed to barely dodge it.
"None of my hits have landed for a while now... Hey, do you practice some kind of martial art?"
"I don't do anything, though?"
"Hmm, you have pretty good reflexes for someone who doesn't..."
That was a lie.
Behind the scenes, I was actually having Professor Voldia read Christina's movements.
『Sōta, am I not amazing!』
(Yeah, yeah, amazing, amazing.)
While I continued dodging half-heartedly, Christina's barrage of attacks suddenly stopped.
"This is boring when I can't hit you at all. I'm over it."
Christina tossed her gloves aside, looking bored.
Now that I think about it, Christina did have a tendency to get bored easily... Well, whatever, at least my time as a punching bag is ov—
"...Just kidding!"
At that moment, a god-speed fist was unleashed.
...Too close.
I barely managed to catch the fist.
"Tch... So a surprise attack doesn't work either..."
"Are you trying to kill me!"
"Did you figure it out?"
Christina smiled mischievously.
Ah, Christina is cute... That alone is enough to forgive everything.
"This time I'm actually done. Thanks for keeping me company, I guess. It was fun."
"Glad to hear it..."
I'm completely exhausted on my end.
『Sōta, do not forget my reward.』
(Yeah, yeah.)
Let's hurry up and go home to rest...
"Ah, right. I have to say this one thing..."
As if suddenly remembering, Christina turned back around.
"You seem to be getting along well with Reina lately... but if you lay a hand on my companion... I'll kill you..."
Leaving only those words behind, Christina went home.
...Ah... This right here... This is Christina...!
Her actions were 100% true to character, leaving me completely satisfied.
After school, having finished my duties as Student Council President, I was heading to a certain building.
Upon arriving, I boarded the elevator, took a card out of my pocket, and held it over the scanner.
The existence of us magical girls is considered top secret.

The 'Artemis Group' is a foundation that supports us magical girls financially and in various other aspects. It is a massive corporation founded by one of the first-generation magical girls, and its name is known worldwide. This is one of the buildings owned by the group.
As a rule, entry by unauthorized personnel is strictly prohibited. Only key figures who know our true identities or my companions are allowed inside.
The elevator ascended to the top floor, an area restricted solely to magical girls, and as the doors opened, our headquarters came into view.
"Sorry I'm late."
The other four were already gathered inside the room.
"You're late, Reina."
"I was faster today."
"Now, now, you two. Reina has Student Council duties as well, you know."
"Yeah, we actually just got here ourselves, so it's totally fine!"
And so, all five of us magical girls, including myself, were gathered.
However, we weren't transformed; we were all wearing our standard Ryūsei Academy uniforms.
Today was our weekly magical girl meeting. Usually, it ends with just some casual idle chat, but today there wasn't a shred of that atmosphere.
"Alright, since everyone is here, let's begin."
As soon as everyone took their seats, our leader, Yui, announced the main topic of today's meeting.
"Today's main topic is about the unidentified man clad in a black robe who appeared the other day—'Omega'. We need to decide how to handle him going forward!"
Just as I expected, today's agenda was about that man, Omega.
I knew it... of course.

That power is far too immense to just leave alone; it's far too dangerous. Normally, as a magical girl maintaining the peace of the citizens, anything that poses even the slightest potential threat should be eliminated.
...I understand that.
But I just couldn't bring myself to think of him as a bad person.
"What are your thoughts, Leader?"

"Hmm... personally, I'd prefer not to become hostile. Especially since there's no actual evidence of Omega doing anything wrong yet. Since we don't know his objective, wouldn't it be best to just wait and see for now?"
"Leader, isn't that way too carefree? It'll be too late once actual damage is done."
Ayano voiced a negative opinion in response to Yui's suggestion.
"That's right. We are magical girls possessing chosen power. We have a duty to protect the peace of the world."
Yūna seemed to share the same opinion. Two opposing voices had already surfaced so quickly, making me feel anxious internally.
But... those two...
"Both of you... are getting too heated... Calm down a little."
Mio, looking as unmotivated as always, firmly stepped in to mediate so the atmosphere wouldn't completely shatter.
Having someone who can bluntly say the things our Leader Yui can't is incredibly important.
"S-Sorry..."
"I apologize, Yui..."
"No, it's fine. I understand both of your points very well. More importantly, I want to hear from Reina, who actually saw Omega in person."
"M-Me?"
"Yeah, I think your words will be the key to our decision."
My words...
Thinking that made my chest feel tight.
That man is a benefactor.
He saved me, and he saved my friends.
I don't want to repay his kindness with hostility.
I—
"I believe that man is an ally."
The two who opposed him cast suspicious glances at me, as if doubting my sanity.
Even so, I continued.
"That person saved us from that demon, you know? Without that person, without Omega, we would have all died back there."
"Well... that's true, but..."
"You're right... If he hadn't come, we would have..."
Ayano and Yūna accepted my words as facts.
"...Alright then. So for now, we'll decide based on Omega's next move. Is everyone okay with that?"
"Yeah..."
"No... objections..."
"Yeah..."
I was overjoyed in my heart that he wasn't deemed an enemy for the time being.
Omega... If I ever meet him again, I want to say thank you...
